attacking_style: Breakaway specialist who targets early moves on mountain stages to collect KOM points rather than stage wins. Willing to trade primary sprint domestique role for classification opportunities.
team_tactics: Cannondale-Drapac grants freedom for breakaway hunting and classification chasing over strict GC protection duties. Team prioritizes opportunistic stage hunting and allows virtual race lead situations in breaks.
terrain_preference: Mountain stages where breakaways can succeed and KOM points are available. Shows unexpected climbing resilience to stay with GC groups on harder alpine terrain.
vulnerability: Limited observations suggest sprint support role conflicts with personal ambitions. Unlikely to contend for stage wins when breakaway is caught.
key_patterns: Targets breakaways on mountain stages for KOM points rather than stage victories (2 instances)
Team grants tactical freedom to pursue classifications over traditional domestique duties (2 instances)
Climbing form stronger than expected, staying with GC groups on Tour stages (2 instances)
form_trajectory: positive: demonstrated better-than-anticipated climbing ability at Tour de France, successfully transitioning from sprint support to mountain breakaway role
